task:
Implement Path A: Post-hoc sub-clustering for mega-clusters in gid-core infer module.

## Problem
Infomap produces mega-clusters (e.g., 96 files in one component) when import/call edge density is high. Current code handles undersized clusters (orphan reassignment) but has no handling for oversized clusters.

## Solution
Add `split_mega_clusters()` step after `run_clustering()` and before `map_to_components()`:

1. After flat Infomap clustering, scan all clusters for any exceeding `max_cluster_size` threshold
2. For each oversized cluster:
   - Extract subgraph (only internal files + internal edges from the network)
   - Run Infomap again on the subgraph with higher teleportation rate (e.g., 0.10) for better resolution
   - If subgraph produces >1 cluster → replace original mega-cluster with sub-clusters
   - If subgraph produces only 1 cluster → stop (truly monolithic, don't force-split)
   - Recursively check sub-results for remaining oversized clusters
3. Max recursion depth = 3 to prevent infinite loops

## Key Design Decisions
- Threshold: `max(20, file_count / 5)` — no cluster should exceed 20% of total files, minimum 20
- Add `max_cluster_size` field to `ClusterConfig` (Option<usize>, auto-computed if None)
- Sub-graph only keeps internal edges (cross-cluster edges naturally cut — this is correct behavior)
- Higher teleportation rate for sub-clustering (0.10 vs default 0.05) to increase resolution on dense subgraphs
- Log diagnostics when splitting occurs

## Files to modify
- `crates/gid-core/src/infer/clustering.rs` — main implementation + tests
- `crates/gid-core/src/infer/mod.rs` — pass config through if needed

## Verification
- All existing tests must pass
- Add new tests:
  - test_split_mega_cluster: create a graph with one dense 30+ node cluster, verify it gets split
  - test_split_preserves_small_clusters: verify clusters under threshold are untouched
  - test_split_stops_on_monolith: create truly monolithic subgraph, verify recursion stops
  - test_split_max_depth: verify recursion depth limit works
- `cargo test -p gid-core` passes
- `cargo clippy -p gid-core` clean
Project location: /Users/potato/clawd/projects/gid-rs
closure_note: Closed retroactively 2026-04-28: work was completed (ContextQuery, ContextFilters, ContextStats, gid context CLI, split_mega_clusters all merged); ritual state never updated.
